1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم حم
1.english: Ha, Meem.
1.transliteration: Haa-Meeem

1.arabic: والكتاب المبين
1.english: By the clear Book,
1.transliteration: Wal Kitaabil Mubeen

1.arabic: إنا جعلناه قرآنا عربيا لعلكم تعقلون
1.english: Indeed, We have made it an Arabic Qur'an that you might understand.
1.transliteration: Innaa ja'alnaahu Quraanan 'Arabiyyal la'allakum ta'qiloon

1.arabic: وإنه في أم الكتاب لدينا لعلي حكيم
1.english: And indeed it is, in the Mother of the Book with Us, exalted and full of wisdom.
1.transliteration: Wa innahoo feee Ummil Kitaabi Ladainaa la'aliyyun hakeem

1.arabic: أفنضرب عنكم الذكر صفحا أن كنتم قوما مسرفين
1.english: Then should We turn the message away, disregarding you, because you are a transgressing people?
1.transliteration: Afanadribu 'ankumuz Zikra safhan an kuntum qawmam musrifeen

1.arabic: وكم أرسلنا من نبي في الأولين
1.english: And how many a prophet We sent among the former peoples,
1.transliteration: Wa kam arsalnaa min Nabiyyin fil awwaleen

1.arabic: وما يأتيهم من نبي إلا كانوا به يستهزئون
1.english: But there would not come to them a prophet except that they used to ridicule him.
1.transliteration: Wa maa yaateehim min Nabiyyin illaa kaanoo bihee yasahzi'oon

1.arabic: فأهلكنا أشد منهم بطشا ومضى مثل الأولين
1.english: And We destroyed greater than them in [striking] power, and the example of the former peoples has preceded.
1.transliteration: Fa ahlaknaaa ashadda minhum batshanw wa madaa masalul lawwaleen

1.arabic: ولئن سألتهم من خلق السماوات والأرض ليقولن خلقهن العزيز العليم
1.english: And if you should ask them, "Who has created the heavens and the earth?" they would surely say, "They were created by the Exalted in Might, the Knowing."
1.transliteration: Wa la'in sa altahum man khalaqas samaawaati wal arda la yaqoolunna khalaqa hunnal 'Azeezul 'Aleem

1.arabic: الذي جعل لكم الأرض مهدا وجعل لكم فيها سبلا لعلكم تهتدون
1.english: [The one] who has made for you the earth a bed and made for you upon it roads that you might be guided
1.transliteration: Allazee ja'ala lakumul arda mahdanw wa ja'ala lakum feehaa subulal la'allakum tahtadoon

1.arabic: والذي نزل من السماء ماء بقدر فأنشرنا به بلدة ميتا ۚ كذلك تخرجون
1.english: And who sends down rain from the sky in measured amounts, and We revive thereby a dead land - thus will you be brought forth -
1.transliteration: Wallazee nazzala minas samaaa'i maaa'am biqadarin fa anshamaa bihee baldatam maitaa' kazaalika tukhrajoon

1.arabic: والذي خلق الأزواج كلها وجعل لكم من الفلك والأنعام ما تركبون
1.english: And who created the species, all of them, and has made for you of ships and animals those which you mount.
1.transliteration: Wallazee khalaqal azwaaja kullahaa wa ja'ala lakum minal fulki wal-an'aami maa tarkaboon

1.arabic: لتستووا على ظهوره ثم تذكروا نعمة ربكم إذا استويتم عليه وتقولوا سبحان الذي سخر لنا هذا وما كنا له مقرنين
1.english: That you may settle yourselves upon their backs and then remember the favor of your Lord when you have settled upon them and say. "Exalted is He who has subjected this to us, and we could not have [otherwise] subdued it.
1.transliteration: Litastawoo 'alaa zuhoorihee summa tazkuroo ni'mata Rabbikum izastawaitum 'alaihi wa taqooloo Subhaanal lazee sakhkhara lana haaza wa maa kunnaa lahoo muqrineen

1.arabic: وإنا إلى ربنا لمنقلبون
1.english: And indeed we, to our Lord, will [surely] return."
1.transliteration: Wa innaaa ilaa Rabbinaa lamunqaliboon

1.arabic: وجعلوا له من عباده جزءا ۚ إن الإنسان لكفور مبين
1.english: But they have attributed to Him from His servants a portion. Indeed, man is clearly ungrateful.
1.transliteration: Wa ja'aloo lahoo min 'ibaadihee juz'aa; innal insaana lakafoorum mubeen

1.arabic: أم اتخذ مما يخلق بنات وأصفاكم بالبنين
1.english: Or has He taken, out of what He has created, daughters and chosen you for [having] sons?
1.transliteration: Amit takhaza mimmaa yakhluqu banaatinw wa asfaakum bilbaneen

1.arabic: وإذا بشر أحدهم بما ضرب للرحمن مثلا ظل وجهه مسودا وهو كظيم
1.english: And when one of them is given good tidings of that which he attributes to the Most Merciful in comparison, his face becomes dark, and he suppresses grief.
1.transliteration: Wa izaa bushshira ahaduhum bimaa daraba lir Rahmaani masalan zalla wajhuhoo muswaddanw wa hua kazeem

1.arabic: أومن ينشأ في الحلية وهو في الخصام غير مبين
1.english: So is one brought up in ornaments while being during conflict unevident [attributed to Allah]?
1.transliteration: Awa mai yunashsha'u fil hilyati wa huwa fil khisaami ghairu mubeen

1.arabic: وجعلوا الملائكة الذين هم عباد الرحمن إناثا ۚ أشهدوا خلقهم ۚ ستكتب شهادتهم ويسألون
1.english: And they have made the angels, who are servants of the Most Merciful, females. Did they witness their creation? Their testimony will be recorded, and they will be questioned.
1.transliteration: Wa ja'alul malaaa'ikatal lazeena hum 'ibaadur Rahmaani inaasaa; a shahidoo khalaqhum; satuktabu shahaa datuhum wa yus'aloon

1.arabic: وقالوا لو شاء الرحمن ما عبدناهم ۗ ما لهم بذلك من علم ۖ إن هم إلا يخرصون
1.english: And they said, "If the Most Merciful had willed, we would not have worshipped them." They have of that no knowledge. They are not but falsifying.
1.transliteration: Wa qaaloo law shaaa'ar Rahmaanu maa 'abadnaahum; maa lahum bizaalika min 'ilmin in hum illaa yakhrusoon

1.arabic: أم آتيناهم كتابا من قبله فهم به مستمسكون
1.english: Or have We given them a book before the Qur'an to which they are adhering?
1.transliteration: Am aatainaahum Kitaabam min qablihee fahum bihee mustamsikoon

1.arabic: بل قالوا إنا وجدنا آباءنا على أمة وإنا على آثارهم مهتدون
1.english: Rather, they say, "Indeed, we found our fathers upon a religion, and we are in their footsteps [rightly] guided."
1.transliteration: Bal qaalooo innaa wajadnaaa aabaaa'anaa 'alaaa ummatinw wa innaa 'alaaa aasaarihim muhtadoon

1.arabic: وكذلك ما أرسلنا من قبلك في قرية من نذير إلا قال مترفوها إنا وجدنا آباءنا على أمة وإنا على آثارهم مقتدون
1.english: And similarly, We did not send before you any warner into a city except that its affluent said, "Indeed, we found our fathers upon a religion, and we are, in their footsteps, following."
1.transliteration: Wa kazaalika maaa arsalnaa min qablika fee qaryatim min nazeerin illaa qaala mutrafoohaaa innaa wajadnaaa aabaaa'anaa 'alaaa ummatinw wa innaa 'alaaa aasaarihim muqtadoon

1.arabic: ۞ قال أولو جئتكم بأهدى مما وجدتم عليه آباءكم ۖ قالوا إنا بما أرسلتم به كافرون
1.english: [Each warner] said, "Even if I brought you better guidance than that [religion] upon which you found your fathers?" They said, "Indeed we, in that with which you were sent, are disbelievers."
1.transliteration: Qaala awa law ji'tukum bi ahdaa mimmaa wajattum 'alaihi aabaaa'akum qaalooo innaa bimaaa ursiltum bihee kaafiroon

1.arabic: فانتقمنا منهم ۖ فانظر كيف كان عاقبة المكذبين
1.english: So we took retribution from them; then see how was the end of the deniers.
1.transliteration: Fantaqamnaa minhum fanzur kaifa kaana 'aaqibatul mukazzibeen

1.arabic: وإذ قال إبراهيم لأبيه وقومه إنني براء مما تعبدون
1.english: And [mention, O Muhammad], when Abraham said to his father and his people, "Indeed, I am disassociated from that which you worship
1.transliteration: Wa iz qaala Ibraaheemu liabeehi wa qawmiheee innane baraaa'um mimmaa ta'budo

1.arabic: إلا الذي فطرني فإنه سيهدين
1.english: Except for He who created me; and indeed, He will guide me."
1.transliteration: Illal lazee fataranee innahoo sa yahdeen

1.arabic: وجعلها كلمة باقية في عقبه لعلهم يرجعون
1.english: And he made it a word remaining among his descendants that they might return [to it].
1.transliteration: Wa ja'alahaa Kalimatam baaqiyatan fee 'aqibihee la'al lahum yarji'oon

1.arabic: بل متعت هؤلاء وآباءهم حتى جاءهم الحق ورسول مبين
1.english: However, I gave enjoyment to these [people of Makkah] and their fathers until there came to them the truth and a clear Messenger.
1.transliteration: Bal matta'tu haaa'ulaaa'i wa aabaaa'ahum hattaa jaaa'a humul haqqu wa Rasoolum mubeen

1.arabic: ولما جاءهم الحق قالوا هذا سحر وإنا به كافرون
1.english: But when the truth came to them, they said, "This is magic, and indeed we are, concerning it, disbelievers."
1.transliteration: Wa lammaa jaaa'ahumul haqqu qaaloo haazaa sihrunw wa innaa bihee kaafiroon

1.arabic: وقالوا لولا نزل هذا القرآن على رجل من القريتين عظيم
1.english: And they said, "Why was this Qur'an not sent down upon a great man from [one of] the two cities?"
1.transliteration: Wa qaaloo law laa nuzzila haazal Quraanu 'alaa rajulim minal qaryataini 'azeem

1.arabic: أهم يقسمون رحمت ربك ۚ نحن قسمنا بينهم معيشتهم في الحياة الدنيا ۚ ورفعنا بعضهم فوق بعض درجات ليتخذ بعضهم بعضا سخريا ۗ ورحمت ربك خير مما يجمعون
1.english: Do they distribute the mercy of your Lord? It is We who have apportioned among them their livelihood in the life of this world and have raised some of them above others in degrees [of rank] that they may make use of one another for service. But the mercy of your Lord is better than whatever they accumulate.
1.transliteration: Ahum yaqsimoona rahmata Rabbik; Nahnu qasamnaa bainahum ma'eeshatahum fil hayaatid dunyaa wa rafa'naa ba'dahum fawqa ba'din darajaatil liyattakhiza ba'duhum ba'dan sukhriyyaa; wa rahmatu Rabbika khairum mimmaa yajma'oon

1.arabic: ولولا أن يكون الناس أمة واحدة لجعلنا لمن يكفر بالرحمن لبيوتهم سقفا من فضة ومعارج عليها يظهرون
1.english: And if it were not that the people would become one community [of disbelievers], We would have made for those who disbelieve in the Most Merciful - for their houses - ceilings and stairways of silver upon which to mount
1.transliteration: Wa law laaa any yakoonan naasu ummatanw waahidatal laja'alnaa limany yakfuru bir Rahmaani libu yootihim suqufam min fiddatinw wa ma'aarija 'alaihaa yazharoon

1.arabic: ولبيوتهم أبوابا وسررا عليها يتكئون
1.english: And for their houses - doors and couches [of silver] upon which to recline
1.transliteration: Wa libu yootihim abwaabanw wa sururan 'alaihaa yattaki'oon

1.arabic: وزخرفا ۚ وإن كل ذلك لما متاع الحياة الدنيا ۚ والآخرة عند ربك للمتقين
1.english: And gold ornament. But all that is not but the enjoyment of worldly life. And the Hereafter with your Lord is for the righteous.
1.transliteration: Wa zukhrufaa; wa in kullu zaalika lammaa mataa'ul hayaatid dunyaa; wal aakhiratu 'inda Rabbika lilmuttaqeen

1.arabic: ومن يعش عن ذكر الرحمن نقيض له شيطانا فهو له قرين
1.english: And whoever is blinded from remembrance of the Most Merciful - We appoint for him a devil, and he is to him a companion.
1.transliteration: Wa mai ya'shu 'an zikrir Rahmaani nuqaiyid lahoo Shaitaanan fahuwa lahoo qareen

1.arabic: وإنهم ليصدونهم عن السبيل ويحسبون أنهم مهتدون
1.english: And indeed, the devils avert them from the way [of guidance] while they think that they are [rightly] guided
1.transliteration: Wa innahum la yasuddoo nahum 'anis sabeeli wa yahsaboona annahum muhtadoon

1.arabic: حتى إذا جاءنا قال يا ليت بيني وبينك بعد المشرقين فبئس القرين
1.english: Until, when he comes to Us [at Judgement], he says [to his companion], "Oh, I wish there was between me and you the distance between the east and west - how wretched a companion."
1.transliteration: Hattaaa izaa jaaa'anaa qaala yaa laita bainee wa bainaka bu'dal mashriqaini fabi'sal qareen

1.arabic: ولن ينفعكم اليوم إذ ظلمتم أنكم في العذاب مشتركون
1.english: And never will it benefit you that Day, when you have wronged, that you are [all] sharing in the punishment.
1.transliteration: Wa lai yanfa'akumul Yawma iz zalamtum annakum fil 'azaabi mushtarikoon

1.arabic: أفأنت تسمع الصم أو تهدي العمي ومن كان في ضلال مبين
1.english: Then will you make the deaf hear, [O Muhammad], or guide the blind or he who is in clear error?
1.transliteration: Afa anta tusmi'us summa aw tahdil 'umya wa man kaana fee dalaalim mubeen

1.arabic: فإما نذهبن بك فإنا منهم منتقمون
1.english: And whether [or not] We take you away [in death], indeed, We will take retribution upon them.
1.transliteration: Fa immaa nazhabanna bika fa innaa minhum muntaqimoon

1.arabic: أو نرينك الذي وعدناهم فإنا عليهم مقتدرون
1.english: Or whether [or not] We show you that which We have promised them, indeed, We are Perfect in Ability.
1.transliteration: Aw nuriyannakal lazee wa'adnaahum fa innaa 'alaihim muqtadiroon

1.arabic: فاستمسك بالذي أوحي إليك ۖ إنك على صراط مستقيم
1.english: So adhere to that which is revealed to you. Indeed, you are on a straight path.
1.transliteration: Fastamsik billazeee oohi ya ilaika innaka 'alaa Siraatim Mustaqeem

1.arabic: وإنه لذكر لك ولقومك ۖ وسوف تسألون
1.english: And indeed, it is a remembrance for you and your people, and you [all] are going to be questioned.
1.transliteration: Wa innahoo lazikrul laka wa liqawmika wa sawfa tus'aloon

1.arabic: واسأل من أرسلنا من قبلك من رسلنا أجعلنا من دون الرحمن آلهة يعبدون
1.english: And ask those We sent before you of Our messengers; have We made besides the Most Merciful deities to be worshipped?
1.transliteration: Was'al man arsalnaa min qablika mir Rusulinaaa aja'alnaa min doonir Rahmaani aalihatany yu badoon

1.arabic: ولقد أرسلنا موسى بآياتنا إلى فرعون وملئه فقال إني رسول رب العالمين
1.english: And certainly did We send Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his establishment, and he said, "Indeed, I am the messenger of the Lord of the worlds."
1.transliteration: Wa laqad arsalnaa Moosaa bi aayaatinaaa ilaa Fir'awna wa mala'ihee faqaala innee Rasoolu Rabbil 'aalameen

1.arabic: فلما جاءهم بآياتنا إذا هم منها يضحكون
1.english: But when he brought them Our signs, at once they laughed at them.
1.transliteration: Falammma jaaa'ahum bi aayaatinaaa izaa hum minhaa yadhakoon

1.arabic: وما نريهم من آية إلا هي أكبر من أختها ۖ وأخذناهم بالعذاب لعلهم يرجعون
1.english: And We showed them not a sign except that it was greater than its sister, and We seized them with affliction that perhaps they might return [to faith].
1.transliteration: Wa maa nureehim min aayatin illaa hiya akbaru min ukhtihaa wa akhaznaahum bil'azaabi la'allahum yarji'oon

1.arabic: وقالوا يا أيه الساحر ادع لنا ربك بما عهد عندك إننا لمهتدون
1.english: And they said [to Moses], "O magician, invoke for us your Lord by what He has promised you. Indeed, we will be guided."
1.transliteration: Wa qaaloo yaaa ayyuhas saahirud'u lanaa Rabbaka bimaa 'ahida 'indaka innanaa lamuhtadoon

1.arabic: فلما كشفنا عنهم العذاب إذا هم ينكثون
1.english: But when We removed from them the affliction, at once they broke their word.
1.transliteration: Falammaa kashafnaa 'anhumul 'azaaba izaa hum yankusoon

1.arabic: ونادى فرعون في قومه قال يا قوم أليس لي ملك مصر وهذه الأنهار تجري من تحتي ۖ أفلا تبصرون
1.english: And Pharaoh called out among his people; he said, "O my people, does not the kingdom of Egypt belong to me, and these rivers flowing beneath me; then do you not see?
1.transliteration: Wa naadaa Fir'awnu fee qawmihee qaala yaa qawmi alaisa lee mulku Misra wa haazihil anhaaru tajree min tahtee afalaa tubsiroon

1.arabic: أم أنا خير من هذا الذي هو مهين ولا يكاد يبين
1.english: Or am I [not] better than this one who is insignificant and hardly makes himself clear?
1.transliteration: Am ana khairum min haazal lazee huwa maheenunw wa laa yuakaadu yubeen

1.arabic: فلولا ألقي عليه أسورة من ذهب أو جاء معه الملائكة مقترنين
1.english: Then why have there not been placed upon him bracelets of gold or come with him the angels in conjunction?"
1.transliteration: Falaw laa ulqiya 'alaihi aswiratum min zahabin awjaaa'a ma'ahul malaaa'ikatu muqtarineen

1.arabic: فاستخف قومه فأطاعوه ۚ إنهم كانوا قوما فاسقين
1.english: So he bluffed his people, and they obeyed him. Indeed, they were [themselves] a people defiantly disobedient [of Allah].
1.transliteration: Fastakhaffa qawmahoo fa ataa'ooh; innahum kaanoo qawman faasiqeen

1.arabic: فلما آسفونا انتقمنا منهم فأغرقناهم أجمعين
1.english: And when they angered Us, We took retribution from them and drowned them all.
1.transliteration: Falammaaa aasafoonan taqamnaa minhum fa aghraqnaahum ajma'een

1.arabic: فجعلناهم سلفا ومثلا للآخرين
1.english: And We made them a precedent and an example for the later peoples.
1.transliteration: Faja'alnaahum salafanw wa masalal lil aakhireen

1.arabic: ۞ ولما ضرب ابن مريم مثلا إذا قومك منه يصدون
1.english: And when the son of Mary was presented as an example, immediately your people laughed aloud.
1.transliteration: Wa lammaa duribab nu Maryama masalan izaa qawmu ka minhu yasidoon

1.arabic: وقالوا أآلهتنا خير أم هو ۚ ما ضربوه لك إلا جدلا ۚ بل هم قوم خصمون
1.english: And they said, "Are your gods better, or is he?" They did not present the comparison except for [mere] argument. But, [in fact], they are a people prone to dispute.
1.transliteration: Wa qaalooo 'a-aalihatunaa khairun am hoo; maa daraboohu laka illaa jadalaa; balhum qawmun khasimoon

1.arabic: إن هو إلا عبد أنعمنا عليه وجعلناه مثلا لبني إسرائيل
1.english: Jesus was not but a servant upon whom We bestowed favor, and We made him an example for the Children of Israel.
1.transliteration: In huwa illaa 'abdun an'amnaa 'alaihi wa ja'alnaahu masalal li Baneee Israaa'eel

1.arabic: ولو نشاء لجعلنا منكم ملائكة في الأرض يخلفون
1.english: And if We willed, We could have made [instead] of you angels succeeding [one another] on the earth.
1.transliteration: Wa law nashaaa'u laja'alnaa minkum malaaa'ikatan fil ardi yakhlufoon

1.arabic: وإنه لعلم للساعة فلا تمترن بها واتبعون ۚ هذا صراط مستقيم
1.english: And indeed, Jesus will be [a sign for] knowledge of the Hour, so be not in doubt of it, and follow Me. This is a straight path.
1.transliteration: Wa innahoo la'ilmul lis Saa'ati fala tamtarunna bihaa wattabi'oon; haazaa Siraatum Mustaqeem

1.arabic: ولا يصدنكم الشيطان ۖ إنه لكم عدو مبين
1.english: And never let Satan avert you. Indeed, he is to you a clear enemy.
1.transliteration: Wa laa yasuddan nakumush Shaitaanu innahoo lakum 'aduwwum mubeen

1.arabic: ولما جاء عيسى بالبينات قال قد جئتكم بالحكمة ولأبين لكم بعض الذي تختلفون فيه ۖ فاتقوا الله وأطيعون
1.english: And when Jesus brought clear proofs, he said, "I have come to you with wisdom and to make clear to you some of that over which you differ, so fear Allah and obey me.
1.transliteration: Wa lammaa jaaa'a 'Eesaa bilbaiyinaati qaala qad ji'tukum bil Hikmati wa li-ubaiyina lakum ba'dal lazee takhtalifoona feehi fattaqul laaha wa atee'oon

1.arabic: إن الله هو ربي وربكم فاعبدوه ۚ هذا صراط مستقيم
1.english: Indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is a straight path."
1.transliteration: Innal laaha Huwa Rabbee wa Rabbukum fa'budooh; haaza Siraatum Mustaqeem

1.arabic: فاختلف الأحزاب من بينهم ۖ فويل للذين ظلموا من عذاب يوم أليم
1.english: But the denominations from among them differed [and separated], so woe to those who have wronged from the punishment of a painful Day.
1.transliteration: Fakhtalafal ahzaabu mim bainihim fawailul lillazeena zalamoo min 'azaabi Yawmin aleem

1.arabic: هل ينظرون إلا الساعة أن تأتيهم بغتة وهم لا يشعرون
1.english: Are they waiting except for the Hour to come upon them suddenly while they perceive not?
1.transliteration: Hal yanzuroona illas Saa'ata an taatiyahum baghtatanw wa hum laa yash'uroon

1.arabic: الأخلاء يومئذ بعضهم لبعض عدو إلا المتقين
1.english: Close friends, that Day, will be enemies to each other, except for the righteous
1.transliteration: Al akhillaaa'u Yawma'izim ba'duhum liba'din 'aduwwun illal muttaqeen

1.arabic: يا عباد لا خوف عليكم اليوم ولا أنتم تحزنون
1.english: [To whom Allah will say], "O My servants, no fear will there be concerning you this Day, nor will you grieve,
1.transliteration: Yaa 'ibaadi laa khawfun 'alaikumul Yawma wa laaa antum tahzanoon

1.arabic: الذين آمنوا بآياتنا وكانوا مسلمين
1.english: [You] who believed in Our verses and were Muslims.
1.transliteration: Allazeena aamanoo bi Aayaatinaa wa kaanoo muslimeen

1.arabic: ادخلوا الجنة أنتم وأزواجكم تحبرون
1.english: Enter Paradise, you and your kinds, delighted."
1.transliteration: Udkhulul Jannata antum wa azwaajukum tuhbaroon

1.arabic: يطاف عليهم بصحاف من ذهب وأكواب ۖ وفيها ما تشتهيه الأنفس وتلذ الأعين ۖ وأنتم فيها خالدون
1.english: Circulated among them will be plates and vessels of gold. And therein is whatever the souls desire and [what] delights the eyes, and you will abide therein eternally.
1.transliteration: Yutaafu 'alaihim bishaa fim min zahabinw wa akwaab, wa feehaa maatashtaheehil anfusu wa talazzul a'yunu wa antum feehaa khaalidoon

1.arabic: وتلك الجنة التي أورثتموها بما كنتم تعملون
1.english: And that is Paradise which you are made to inherit for what you used to do.
1.transliteration: Wa tilkal jannatul lateee ooristumoohaa bimaa kuntum ta'maloon

1.arabic: لكم فيها فاكهة كثيرة منها تأكلون
1.english: For you therein is much fruit from which you will eat.
1.transliteration: Lakum feehaa faakihatun kaseeratum minhaa taakuloon

1.arabic: إن المجرمين في عذاب جهنم خالدون
1.english: Indeed, the criminals will be in the punishment of Hell, abiding eternally.
1.transliteration: Innal mujrimeena fee 'azaabi jahannama khaalidoon

1.arabic: لا يفتر عنهم وهم فيه مبلسون
1.english: It will not be allowed to subside for them, and they, therein, are in despair.
1.transliteration: Laa yufattaru 'anhum wa hum feehi mublisoon

1.arabic: وما ظلمناهم ولكن كانوا هم الظالمين
1.english: And We did not wrong them, but it was they who were the wrongdoers.
1.transliteration: Wa maa zalamnaahum wa laakin kaanoo humuz zaalimeen

1.arabic: ونادوا يا مالك ليقض علينا ربك ۖ قال إنكم ماكثون
1.english: And they will call, "O Malik, let your Lord put an end to us!" He will say, "Indeed, you will remain."
1.transliteration: Wa naadaw yaa Maaliku liyaqdi 'alainaa Rabbuka qaala innakum maakisson

1.arabic: لقد جئناكم بالحق ولكن أكثركم للحق كارهون
1.english: We had certainly brought you the truth, but most of you, to the truth, were averse.
1.transliteration: Laqad ji'naakum bilhaqqi wa laakinna aksarakum lilhaqqi kaarihoon

1.arabic: أم أبرموا أمرا فإنا مبرمون
1.english: Or have they devised [some] affair? But indeed, We are devising [a plan].
1.transliteration: Am abramooo amran fainnaa mubrimoon

1.arabic: أم يحسبون أنا لا نسمع سرهم ونجواهم ۚ بلى ورسلنا لديهم يكتبون
1.english: Or do they think that We hear not their secrets and their private conversations? Yes, [We do], and Our messengers are with them recording.
1.transliteration: Am yahsaboona annaa laa nasma'u sirrahum wa najwaahum; balaa wa Rusulunaa ladaihim yaktuboon

1.arabic: قل إن كان للرحمن ولد فأنا أول العابدين
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], "If the Most Merciful had a son, then I would be the first of [his] worshippers."
1.transliteration: Qul in kaana lir Rahmaani walad; fa-ana awwalul 'aabideen

1.arabic: سبحان رب السماوات والأرض رب العرش عما يصفون
1.english: Exalted is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Lord of the Throne, above what they describe.
1.transliteration: Subhaana Rabbis samaawaati wal ardi Rabbil Arshi 'ammaa yasifoon

1.arabic: فذرهم يخوضوا ويلعبوا حتى يلاقوا يومهم الذي يوعدون
1.english: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised.
1.transliteration: Fazarhum yakhoodoo wa yal'aboo hattaa yulaaqoo Yawmahumul lazee yoo'adoon

1.arabic: وهو الذي في السماء إله وفي الأرض إله ۚ وهو الحكيم العليم
1.english: And it is Allah who is [the only] deity in the heaven, and on the earth [the only] deity. And He is the Wise, the Knowing.
1.transliteration: Wa Huwal lazee fissamaaa'i Ilaahunw wa fil ardi Ilaah; wa Huwal Hakeemul'Aleem

1.arabic: وتبارك الذي له ملك السماوات والأرض وما بينهما وعنده علم الساعة وإليه ترجعون
1.english: And blessed is He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them and with whom is knowledge of the Hour and to whom you will be returned.
1.transliteration: Wa tabaarakal lazee lahoo mulkus samaawaati wal ardi wa maa bainahumaa wa 'indahoo 'ilmus Saa'ati wa ilaihi turja'oon

1.arabic: ولا يملك الذين يدعون من دونه الشفاعة إلا من شهد بالحق وهم يعلمون
1.english: And those they invoke besides Him do not possess [power of] intercession; but only those who testify to the truth [can benefit], and they know.
1.transliteration: Wa laa yamlikul lazeena yad'oona min doonihish shafaa'ata illaa man shahida bilhaqqi wa hum ya'lamoon

1.arabic: ولئن سألتهم من خلقهم ليقولن الله ۖ فأنى يؤفكون
1.english: And if you asked them who created them, they would surely say, "Allah." So how are they deluded?
1.transliteration: Wa la'in sa altahum man khalaqahum la yaqoolun nallaahu fa annaa yu'fakoon

1.arabic: وقيله يا رب إن هؤلاء قوم لا يؤمنون
1.english: And [Allah acknowledges] his saying, "O my Lord, indeed these are a people who do not believe."
1.transliteration: Wa qeelihee yaa Rabbi inna haa'ulaaa'i qawmul laa yu'minoon

1.arabic: فاصفح عنهم وقل سلام ۚ فسوف يعلمون
1.english: So turn aside from them and say, "Peace." But they are going to know.
1.transliteration: Fasfah 'anhum wa qul salaam; fasawfa ya'lamoon
